title: Global Semiconductor Sales Increase 9.2% Month-to-Month in May
gist: Global semiconductor sales hit an all-time monthly record of $120.6B in May 2026, up 9.2% month-over-month and 104.1% year-over-year, marking the 15th consecutive month of sequential growth led by the Americas, Asia-Pacific, and China.

insights.claim: Global semiconductor sales reached $120.6 billion in May 2026, the highest-ever recorded monthly sales total for the industry.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: An all-time record confirms the sector is in a demand super-cycle; watch for peak-cycle signals but the trend still favors long semiconductor exposure.
insights.quote: hitting the highest-ever recorded monthly sales total
insights.locator: The global semiconductor market continued to grow

insights.claim: Semiconductor sales grew 104.1% year-over-year in May 2026, more than doubling from the $59.1 billion recorded in May 2025.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A 2x YoY jump is a violent inflection that lifts equipment, foundry, and memory names; base effects will make future YoY comps harder to sustain.
insights.quote: 104.1% more than the May 2025 total of $59.1 billion

insights.claim: May 2026 sales rose 9.2% month-over-month from April's $110.5 billion, the 15th consecutive month of sequential growth.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Fifteen straight months of MoM gains signals durable momentum rather than a one-off spike; a break in this streak would be an early cycle-rollover warning.
insights.quote: increasing on a month-to-month basis for the 15th consecutive month in May
insights.locator: The global semiconductor market continued to grow

insights.claim: The Americas led year-over-year semiconductor sales growth at 132.2%, ahead of Asia Pacific/All Other at 118.9%, China at 88.8%, Europe at 60.7%, and Japan at 23.8%.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Americas outperformance points to AI/datacenter demand concentration; overweight US-exposed suppliers while treating Japan's 23.8% as the laggard region.
insights.quote: up in the Americas (132.2%), Asia Pacific/All Other (118.9%), China (88.8%), Europe (60.7%), and Japan (23.8%)

insights.claim: China posted the fastest month-over-month sales growth at 10.7%, ahead of Asia Pacific/All Other (9.2%), the Americas (8.6%), Europe (7.3%), and Japan (6.4%).
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: China leading sequential growth despite lower YoY suggests accelerating near-term restocking or localization demand; relevant for firms with China exposure amid export-control risk.
insights.quote: increased in China (10.7%), Asia Pacific/All Other (9.2%), the Americas (8.6%), Europe (7.3%), and Japan (6.4%)

insights.claim: SIA attributes ongoing sales growth specifically to strong year-to-year expansion in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific region, and China rather than a broad-based global lift.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Growth concentration in three regions means the rally is exposed to region-specific policy or demand shocks; monitor those geographies for cycle turning points.
insights.quote: Sales growth continues to be driven by strong year-to-year growth in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific region, and China.
insights.locator: The global semiconductor market continued to grow

insights.claim: The reported monthly semiconductor sales figures represent a three-month moving average compiled by the World Semiconductor Trade Statistics (WSTS) organization.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Because figures are smoothed three-month averages, single-month prints lag true inflections; treat them as trend confirmation, not real-time turning-point signals.
insights.quote: Monthly sales are compiled by the World Semiconductor Trade Statistics (WSTS) organization and represent a three-month moving average.
